tool_args.label: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS

payload.title: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.prepared_for: May 2023 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee

evidence_refs.source_quote: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
May 2023
This performance metrics document was prepared for the May 2023 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Frank Annicaro, Senior Vice President of Buses, NYCT

evidence_refs.source_quote: On buses, you heard from Senior Vice President Frank Annicaro and his team last month about their laser-focused plan to improve 29 priority routes.

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.
entity.office is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Office names are relation context unless the source treats the office as the entity.

evidence_refs.source_quote: I wanted to shout out Senior Vice President Demetrius Crichlow and his team for two major ridership milestones in the past month.

evidence_refs.source_quote: This performance metrics document was prepared for the May 2023 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee.

tool_args.label: Jose LaSalle, Weekend Service Czar, NYCT

evidence_refs.source_quote: Weekend Service Czar Jose LaSalle and his team's exhaustive work to make service better and more reliable for customers

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Vice President Chris Pangilinan and his entire team

evidence_refs.source_quote: coordinating with our partners at NYC DOT to add bus lanes and busways
NYCT continues to partner with NYPD and MTAPD
NYPD Transit Bureau
MTAPD continues to focus their efforts in the subway system at 4 major hubs

tool_args.label: ABLE Bus Lane Enforcement Camera Program

payload.project_name: ABLE Bus Lane Enforcement Camera Program
payload.description: Automated bus lane enforcement (ABLE) camera program uses cameras to enforce bus lanes, speeding up buses by an additional 5% on priority corridors and reducing collisions

evidence_refs.source_quote: The ABLE camera program is a double win for bus customers. Not only do ABLE cams succeed in speeding up buses by an additional 5% on priority corridors, but they can also reduce collisions
This year the MTA will more than double the number of buses equipped with ABLE from 423 at the end of last year to over 1,000 at the end of this year

payload.description: Station renovation program using planned weekend shutdowns to overhaul stations. 29 stations renovated so far, on pace to complete 50 by end of 2023.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 29 stations have been re-NEW-vated so far, and we are on pace to complete 50 by the end of 2023
29 Station ReNEWvations

payload.description: Borough Bus Network Redesign implemented in June 2022 in the Bronx. Early results showed improvements in speed, frequency, and connections on local bus routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the improvements in bus performance we have seen in the Bronx following last June's implementation of the Bronx Local Bus Network Redesign

tool_args.label: Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Draft Plan

payload.description: Draft Plan published; 15 in-person public events held between April 14 and May 11, 2023; 18 virtual workshops held January-March 2023

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign Team invited Brooklyn bus riders to share their feedback and provide input on the Brooklyn Bus Network Redesign (BkBNR) Draft Plan at 15 in-person public events this spring.
18 virtual workshops held between January and March 2023

payload.description: Open Stroller pilot program launched September 2022, will expand to involve roughly 1,000 buses on 50 routes by end of 2023

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Open Stroller pilot program, which was successfully launched last September, will soon expand, and involve roughly 1,000 buses on 50 routes by the end of this year.

payload.description: Received $2M U.S. DOT SMART Grant in March 2023 to fund expanded testing of NaviLens wayfinding app. Previously tested at Jay St-MetroTech station and along M23 bus route.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the MTA received one of the first U.S. DOT's SMART Grants , a $2M award to fund expanded testing of the NaviLens wayfinding app

payload.description: Wide Aisle Gates (WAGs) installed at Atlantic Ave-Barclays Center and Sutphin Blvd-Archer Av-JFK Airport stations. First month in service was April 2023. Average of about 1,500 customers using them daily.

evidence_refs.source_quote: April was the first month that our two new Wide Aisle Gates were in service... installed at Atlantic Ave-Barclays Center and Sutphin Blvd-Archer Av-JFK Airport stations... average of about 1,500 customers dipping their MetroCards or tapping with OMNY daily

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

payload.description: Crosstown route, carrying approximately 19,000 weekday riders with key connections to 22 bus routes and the 1, A, 4, B, D subway lines. First ABLE route of 2023.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the MTA rolled out the first of our 2023 ABLE routes, the Bx35. The Bx35 is an essential crosstown route for the Bronx, carrying approximately 19,000 weekday riders with key connections to 22 bus routes and the 1, A, 4, B, D subway lines.

payload.description: Wide Aisle Gates (WAGs) installed at Atlantic Ave-Barclays Center and Sutphin Blvd-Archer Av-JFK Airport stations. First month in service was April 2023. Average of about 1,500 customers using them daily.

evidence_refs.source_quote: April was the first month that our two new Wide Aisle Gates were in service
The overall subway experience satisfaction rate for April is 62%, which is unchanged from March.
Overall bus experience satisfaction in April remains at 69%, matching March's score and is a five-percentage point increase since December.
Access-A-Ride (Paratransit) overall satisfaction in April reached an all-time high of 78%
Weekday OTP was 85.0% -- again higher than any month in 2022 or 2019.
Weekend OTP also climbed to 84.7%, which is consistent with performance thus far in 2023

payload.description: share of customer trips with total travel time within 5 minutes of scheduled time

evidence_refs.source_quote: We delivered 85.7% of customers to their destinations within 5 minutes of schedule, including waiting and on-board time

tool_args.label: Average Customer Additional Journey Time April 2023

payload.description: Average customer's additional journey time

evidence_refs.source_quote: the average customer's additional journey time was 98 seconds, or 14 seconds faster than April 2022 and 15 seconds faster than April 2019.
carrying approximately 19,000 weekday riders

warnings: metric_claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids on metric payloads refer to route entities and should become explicit relations when supported. Suggested relation: route has_metric metric_claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: Bus Customer Journey Time Performance April 2023

evidence_refs.source_quote: Customer Journey Time Performance (CJTP) was 75.2% in April
Bus speeds averaged 8.2 mph, a 2.5% improvement compared to pre-pandemic and 1.2% improvement over last month and last year.

tool_args.label: Subway Average Paid Weekday Ridership March 2023

evidence_refs.source_quote: Average paid weekday subway ridership dropped by less than 1% percent in March, from 3.685 million to 3.657 million
midweek ridership is now approaching that 4 million threshold, at 3.989 million

payload.description: New R-211 subway cars entered service on the A line

evidence_refs.source_quote: in March, we were thrilled to welcome the new R-211 into service on the A line
